author: lOrm
content:
Resolved. Changes to `src/web.rs`:

**New machinery:**
- `DANGEROUS_HOSTNAMES` const slice: `localhost`, `localhost.localdomain`, `ip6-localhost`, `ip6-loopback`, plus cloud-metadata endpoints (`metadata.google.internal`, `metadata.goog`, `metadata.aws`, `metadata.tencentyun.com`, `instance-data`, `instance-data.ec2.internal`) and Kubernetes in-cluster hosts (`kubernetes`, `kubernetes.default`, `kubernetes.default.svc`, full FQDN).
- `parse_host_as_ip(host)`: recognizes standard dotted-quad IPv4, bracketed IPv6, **decimal-integer IPv4** (`2130706433` → 127.0.0.1), and **hex-integer IPv4** (`0x7f000001`).
- `is_ipv6_forbidden(v6)`: unwraps IPv4-mapped form and re-checks as IPv4; then denies unique-local (fc00::/7), link-local (fe80::/10), 6to4 (2002::/16), and Teredo (2001:0000::/32).
- `is_ip_forbidden(ip)`: full IPv4 matrix via `Ipv4Addr` stdlib methods plus manual ranges — loopback, private, link-local, broadcast, unspecified, multicast, documentation, 100.64/10 CGNAT, 192.0.0/24 IETF, 198.18/15 benchmarking, 240.0.0.0/4 reserved.

**Rewritten `validate_url`:**
1. Scheme allowlist (http/https).
2. Hostname denylist match.
3. IP-literal match via `parse_host_as_ip` — handles decimal/hex encoding.
4. DNS resolution via `ToSocketAddrs`; **every** resolved address checked against `is_ip_forbidden`.

**All QA-listed bypasses closed:**
- Bypass 1 (hostnames): `metadata.google.internal` now explicitly denylisted + DNS-resolution check.
- Bypass 2 (DNS rebinding): **partially** mitigated — validate-time check catches unsophisticated rebinds; full defense needs a custom `reqwest::Resolver` that re-checks at dial time (tracked as a follow-up).
- Bypass 3 (decimal IPv4): `parse_host_as_ip` decodes and classifies.
- Bypass 4 (IPv6 partial): full matrix now — mapped, ULA, link-local, 6to4, Teredo all blocked.
- Bypass 5 (bogus 172/2 prefix): now uses `Ipv4Addr::is_private()` which correctly implements 172.16.0.0/12. Added test `ssrf_previous_prefix_bug_172_200_now_allowed` to lock the fix against regression.

**Tests:** 15 new SSRF regression tests in `web::tests` (ssrf_* prefix) covering decimal/hex-encoded loopback, GCP/AWS metadata hostnames, K8s control plane, IPv6-mapped loopback, IPv6 ULA + link-local, CGNAT range, documentation range, future-reserved 240/4, benchmarking 198.18/15, public 172.200.x acceptance, public 8.8.8.8 acceptance, and all five private 172.x middle-octets (16,20,25,28,31). Total lib test count: 574 → 589, all passing. 130 integration tests also still pass.

**Out of scope / follow-up:**
- Custom `reqwest::Resolver` wrapping `TokioResolver` that enforces the same `is_ip_forbidden` check at each dial, closing the DNS-rebinding residual. Filed as a successor issue is advisable.
- URL normalization (look-alike unicode hosts, percent-encoded dots, etc.) — url crate already handles most of this; further hardening should live in a separate refactor.
